message:
feat(io): CSV true_values / false_values parity with pandas (FP-P2D-426)

Wire the pandas-parity `true_values` and `false_values` CSV reader
options through fp-io, plumb them into the conformance harness so
they are driven by fixture packets, and lock the strict-mode
behavior with a new golden fixture.

crates/fp-io/src/lib.rs:
- Extend CsvReadOptions with two new Vec<String> fields: `true_values`
  and `false_values`, documented to match pandas' read_csv params.
  They default to empty Vecs so existing callers get identical
  behavior to before.
- Teach parse_scalar_with_options to consider these two lists
  immediately after na-filter short-circuiting and before the
  int/float attempts, so an exact match against one of the caller-
  supplied tokens coerces to Scalar::Bool(true|false). This ordering
  matches pandas: boolean coercion beats numeric coercion when a
  value appears in either list.
- Thread the two new slices through append_csv_record so the
  per-field parse sees the options without a global.

crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs:
- Extend PacketFixture with `csv_true_values: Option<Vec<String>>`
  and `csv_false_values: Option<Vec<String>>` (both #[serde(default)])
  so existing fixtures keep deserializing unchanged while new
  fixtures can specify custom truthy/falsy tokens.
- Pipe those fields into execute_csv_read_frame_fixture_operation's
  CsvReadOptions construction via clone().unwrap_or_default() so the
  runtime sees empty Vecs when the fixture omits the key.

crates/fp-conformance/fixtures/packets/fp_p2d_426_csv_read_frame_true_false_values_strict.json:
- New strict-mode fixture locking the contract: input "flag\n1\n0\n"
  with csv_true_values=["1"], csv_false_values=["0"] must produce a
  single-column bool frame `[true, false]` with the default Int64
  row index. Mirrors the canonical pandas behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1643,6 +1643,10 @@ pub struct PacketFixture {
     #[serde(default)]
     pub csv_on_bad_lines: Option<String>,
     #[serde(default)]
+    pub csv_true_values: Option<Vec<String>>,
+    #[serde(default)]
+    pub csv_false_values: Option<Vec<String>>,
+    #[serde(default)]
     pub json_input: Option<String>,
     #[serde(default)]
     pub json_orient: Option<String>,
@@ -11670,6 +11674,8 @@ fn execute_csv_read_frame_fixture_operation(fixture: &PacketFixture) -> Result<D
     let options = CsvReadOptions {
         decimal,
         on_bad_lines,
+        true_values: fixture.csv_true_values.clone().unwrap_or_default(),
+        false_values: fixture.csv_false_values.clone().unwrap_or_default(),
         ..CsvReadOptions::default()
     };
     read_csv_with_options(csv_input, &options).map_err(|err| format!("csv read failed: {err}"))
